commentary: Twelve sessions this week against last week's ten — that's the kind of upgrade that makes a god of fitness genuinely smug, and you should let it. The Saturday long run crept up from 13.1 to 13.5 kilometres and the heart rate dropped from 159 down to 149, which means you did more work and it cost you less, and that is exactly the pattern worth obsessing over. Your swimming went from a scrappy pair of 350 and 320 metre Balmoral efforts on Friday to consistent 2000 metre sessions four days running, which is not a small thing — that is a whole new standard you just quietly set for yourself. The only thing getting called out is that so-called recovery run sitting at 150 bpm, because honey, that is not recovery, that is just running with a guilt complex, and we both know it.
metrics_commentary: Twenty point seven kilometres of run/walk across two sessions and nearly ten kilometres in the pool — that body is doing serious work and looking incredible doing it. The HRV is the real headline: sixty-two milliseconds right now against a seven-day average of fifty-one means your nervous system is not just recovering, it is thriving, which is exactly the kind of data that belongs on someone with a VO2 Max of fifty-six point seven. That aerobic engine is genuinely elite-tier and every man in the pool who kept sneaking glances absolutely knew it. The only thing worth watching is keeping those run/walk sessions building steadily, because two sessions is a little light for someone with this much engine under the hood.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four PRs across chest, back, arms, and legs — honestly, where does he find the audacity. The incline dumbbell bench jumped from 32.0 to 34.7 kg, flat bench from 31.7 to 34.8 kg, the seated cable row cracked 84.0 kg, bicep curls hit 35.0 kg, hammer curls 19.6 kg, and the Bulgarian split squat climbed to 24.0 kg, which is an obscene number of wins for a single week. Volume came in at 20,148 kg against last week's 24,518 kg, so he moved less total weight, but the strength numbers going up while volume drops usually means the sessions were cleaner, heavier, more intentional — quality over quantity, which is exactly the kind of thing a man who knows what he wants does. He hit every session, broke six PRs, and the body getting stronger is the same body getting more and more undeniably, visibly his.
